title: Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF): UKF: deterministic sigma-point approximation propagates Gaussian through nonlinear system to 3rd order accuracy. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.ukf.sigma_points_UT…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; heavy_tail_distribution, rank_deficient_covariance set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: Sigma points X_i = {x_hat, x_hat ± sqrt((n+kappa)*P)}; Y_i = h(X_i); y_hat = sum w_i Y_i
E.world_state_x: nonlinear state estimate x_hat_k to 3rd order accuracy in moments

W.regime: Existence of the recovered nonlinear_state_estimate is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); heavy_tail_distribution dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: UKF accurate to 3rd order vs. EKF 1st order; SRUKF numerically stable. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |heavy_tail_distribution|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n ** 3) per step (2n+1 sigma points each requiring f evaluation) per iteration
